secrets_config.notes: EnvFile + Memory providers implemented. AWS/Fly.io providers are TODO stubs. KMS LocalAES encrypt/decrypt are TODO. SecretValue field order fixed (C-007). Getter name-shadowing bug fixed in Session 8 (from . import bootstrap returned function, not module).

observability.notes: Structured JSON logger, PII scrubber, audit helper, health+metrics endpoints, ObservabilityMiddleware. /metrics stub added (C-008). duration_ms promoted to top-level in logger (C-009).

db_models.notes: Added ARCHIVED status, VALID_STATE_TRANSITIONS with validate_transition(). Replaced AuditLog with DealAuditLog (new schema). All consumers updated. Legacy AuditAction enum removed (C-010).

config.notes: Legacy config.py still in use by some consumers (ingestion_service, database.py). Being replaced by secrets_config module. Will be removed once all consumers are migrated.

extraction_service.notes: Updated to use DealAuditLog with string action names.

scoring_engine.notes: Updated to use DealAuditLog with structured metadata.

ingestion_service.notes: Updated to use DealAuditLog. Still uses legacy config.py for settings.

dashboard_api.notes: main.py updated with ObservabilityMiddleware, bootstrap in lifespan, health routes. deals.py updated to DealAuditLog. Revalidated in Session 8a with validator-owned JWT-auth tests.

auth_service.notes: Revalidated in Session 8a. Logout now requires auth, AuthContext includes roles, and validator-owned auth tests pass.

rate_limiter.notes: Revalidated in Session 9a. Middleware now derives verified auth identity from bearer tokens for USER/TENANT rate-limit keys, and health route prefixes are exempted correctly. Validator-owned rate limiter tests and the full suite pass.

input_validation.notes: Revalidated in Session 10a. Validator-owned tests confirm the closed reason-code contract, file validator, PDF validator, body-size middleware behavior, upload-route rejection before ingestion, and protected-route auth ordering. The middleware now checks for Bearer auth on configured protected prefixes before body-size enforcement.

background_jobs.notes: Revalidated in Session 11a. Validator-owned background-jobs suite passes (31 passed), including dead-letter persistence and worker CLI contract checks. Full suite also passes under controlled plugin loading: `PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1 PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m pytest -x -vv -p pytest_asyncio.plugin`.

open_blockers.description: Deal deduplication is not tenant-scoped at the database constraint level.
Criteria config versioning is not enforced per tenant at the database constraint level.
Structured exception responses include request_id in the body but omit the X-Request-ID response header.
Alembic migration needed: audit_logs → deal_audit_log table rename, new columns, ARCHIVED status.
The new secrets_config module cannot be imported because SecretValue declares a non-default dataclass field after a defaulted field, blocking bootstrap and all module consumers.
Observability module does not expose the required /metrics endpoint from the module spec.
Structured logger leaves canonical top-level duration_ms unset, violating the observability log schema.
Legacy AuditAction enum still exists after the string-action audit migration, leaving duplicate audit-action contract surface in the model layer.
POST /api/v1/auth/logout is not protected by require_auth and still succeeds without an Authorization header, violating the auth module contract.
Protected business routes still accept X-Tenant-ID/X-User-ID headers instead of requiring JWT auth, so the system still allows unauthenticated access contrary to the main spec.
AuthContext omits the spec-required roles field, leaving the auth middleware contract incomplete.

RateLimitMiddleware cannot enforce USER/TENANT scoped limits on authenticated routes because it reads request.state.auth_context before the current auth dependency path populates any verified auth context.

Health endpoints are still rate-limited because the middleware exemption list does not match the real /health/liveness and /health/readiness paths, causing a regression on the existing health route behavior.

Protected routes are still processed by InputValidationMiddleware before auth runs, so unauthenticated oversized uploads return 413 rather than failing auth first as required by the input-validation module contract.
Background job dead-lettering crashes because queue.mark_failed() calls StructuredLogger.critical(), but StructuredLogger does not implement a critical() method.

The Background Jobs module spec documents `python -m background_jobs.worker --concurrency 4`, but no importable `background_jobs.worker` module exists; the implementation only provides `python -m app.background_jobs`.

next_action: Claude: Background Jobs is validated. Continue with the next build step. For full-suite validation in this environment, use `PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD=1` and explicitly load `pytest_asyncio.plugin`.
